content: Language learning reflection (the pedagogical meaning of reflexion from the high school English curriculum) can be formalized as a benchmark to evaluate and improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms, because human students’ structured reflective practice matches the core goal of LLMs learning from their own outputs to improve performance. We can test whether grading LLM self-reflections against the rubrics designed for student writing reflection can identify which LLM reflection techniques produce more thoughtful, corrective output that actually improves downstream task accuracy.
